celerity.ex: The emergency team responded to the call with great celerity.

celery.ex: She added some chopped crunchy celery to the chicken salad.

celesta.ex: The soft sound of the celesta gave the music a magical feel.

celestial.def: 天空的、神聖的、(古)中國人、神的人的人物、神聖地 (天)
celestial.ex: Early sailors used celestial navigation to find their way home.

celibacy.ex: The priests must take a strict vow of celibacy and poverty.

celibate.def: 獨身的、禁慾的、獨身者、人的人的人物 (獨身)
celibate.ex: He chose to live a quiet and celibate life in the monastery.

cell.def: 細胞、小室、電池、小部分、住在小室中、住在小室的人的人物、電池地 (胞)
cell.col: blood cell, cell phone, prison cell, solar cell
cell.ex: Each human body is made up of millions of different cells.

cellar.def: 地窖、地下室、酒窖、儲藏地窖、地窖的人的人物、儲藏地 (地窖)
cellar.ex: They kept their best vintage wines in a cool, dark cellar.

cellarage.def: 地窖空間、地窖內藏物、地窖租金的人物 (單數)
cellarage.ex: The old house had sufficient cellarage for all their storage.

cellist.ex: The famous cellist performed a stunning solo at the concert.

cello.ex: She has been learning to play the cello since she was seven.

cellophane.ex: The fresh flowers were neatly wrapped in shiny cellophane.

cellular.def: 細胞的、單元的、由細胞組成的的人物 (單數)
cellular.ex: The cellular network coverage in the mountains was very poor.

cellule.ex: The researcher examined a single biological cellule carefully.

celluloid.def: 賽璐珞、電影膠片、電影、虛幻的人物 (單數)
celluloid.ex: Many classic stories have been captured on celluloid forever.

cellulose.ex: Cellulose is the main component of plant cell walls and wood.

celsius.ex: Water freezes at zero degrees Celsius under normal pressure.

celt/kelt.ex: The ancient Celts lived in many parts of Western Europe.

celtic/keltic.def: 凱爾特人的、凱爾特語的的人物 (單數)
celtic/keltic.ex: She loves listening to traditional Celtic music on Saturday.

cement.def: 水泥、膠結劑、牙骨質、鞏固、結合、水泥的人的人物、鞏固地 (密)
cement.col: cement floor, cement mixer, cement the relationship
cement.ex: They used strong cement to fix the loose stones in the wall.

cementation.def: 水泥化、膠結、鞏固、(化)滲碳 (單數)
cementation.ex: The natural cementation of sand created these strange rocks.

cementing.def: 鞏固的、水泥化的的人 (或物) (密)
cementing.ex: The handshake was seen as cementing the deal between them.

cemetery.ex: Flowers were placed on the graves in the local cemetery.

cenotaph.def: 衣冠塚、紀念碑的人物 (單數)
cenotaph.ex: The queen laid a wreath at the national war cenotaph today.

censer.ex: The priest swung the gold censer, filling the air with smoke.

censor.def: 審查員、審查、刪改、監察委員的人的人物、審查地 (審)
censor.ex: The government managed to censor the reports about the riots.

censored.def: 被刪剪的、經過審查的人的人 (或物) (審)
censored.ex: We only saw a heavily censored version of the news tonight.

censorship.def: 審查制度、檢查的人的人物 (單數)
censorship.ex: Strict censorship was imposed on all forms of local media.
